name: Szechwan Cucumber Salad
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons small dried wood ear mushrooms
4 red hot peppers, cut into thin rounds
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour boiling water over the wood ear mushrooms and let stand for 15 minutes.
2. Peel the cucumbers, cut them in half lengthwise, and scrape out the seeds.
3. Slice the cucumbers into thin half-moons.
4. When the mushrooms are soft, drain, rinse, and dry them. If they are too large, slice them.
5. Combine the cucumbers, mushrooms, remaining ingredients (soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, salt, sesame oil), and wood ears in a bowl.
6. Let the mixture stand for 15 minutes, then toss again before serving.
NOTE: Wood ears can be found in Chinese markets. After soaking, they have little taste but lots of crunch. They are there for texture. The substitution options include raw carrots or water chestnuts. Be sure to use dark, cooked Asian sesame oil rather than the light variety commonly available at health food stores. It is best to prepare this dish ahead of time and chill it well while preparing other components.
